indication: Post-op hypotension (SBP 60s) with fever (103F) — differentiate cardiogenic vs septic shock in patient with known EF 20%

pre_procedure_vitals.heart_rhythm: Atrial fibrillation with RVR (130s-140s), spontaneously cardioverted to NSR after lopressor

clinical_context.fluids_given: 4L NS + 1 unit PRBC (Hct 27, mixed venous O2 sat 61%)

PCWP.interpretation: Borderline elevated (normal 6-12) — consistent with baseline heart failure

CO.interpretation: Adequate (normal 4-8) — hyperdynamic for EF 20%, consistent with sepsis

clinical_interpretation: Hemodynamic profile consistent with SEPTIC (distributive) shock, NOT cardiogenic shock. Low SVR is the primary driver of hypotension. Despite severely depressed EF, cardiac output is maintained/elevated due to sepsis-driven vasodilation. Treatment: volume resuscitation + vasopressors.
outcome: Levophed weaned off overnight. Extubated next morning. Transferred to floor on hospital day 4. Discharged to extended care on hospital day 7.
